column1: # @ROOT@ is substituted with the git-sandbox root at run time. Every generic
# rule (guard-kit/SPEC.md §The generic ruleset) has a firing + a non-firing
# case. Columns are tab-separated; # and blank lines are ignored.
# rule 1 — cd in a compound command
# rule 2 — git -C <repo-root> when cwd is the root
# rule 4 — absolute-path read-only repo script (rewrite) vs other script (block)
# rule 5 — repo-root absolute prefix (non-script) steered; foreign path untouched
# rule 6 — shell expansion / assignment; single-quoted $ stays literal
# rule 7 — unquoted brace glyph: bare {} placeholder rewritten, expansions blocked
# rule 8 — sed reading or rewriting a file directly; a piped-into sed is a filter
# rule 9 — listing-only find steered to Glob; action-predicate / piped /
# rule 10 — bare single-file cat steered to Read; multi-file, piped, redirected
#           forms are composition and pass (a lone flagged read still fires)
# rule 11 — working-tree git grep steered to Grep (a path-limited `-- <path>`
#           search is still the working tree); a revision or --cached passes
# rule 12 — `: > file` truncation auto-allowed iff every target is gitignored
# rule 13 — read-only pipeline auto-allow; a non-roster segment falls through
# rule 14 — decorated allowlisted bare lead with an unmatched segment blocked;
#           chaining steer, redirect steer, and the untouched non-firing cases
# rule 15 — git history-rewrite advisory: amend / commit -F / soft-reset steered;
#           a plain commit and a non-soft reset pass
# rule 16 — bare rm of a tracked path steers to 'git rm -q'; untracked,
#           gitignored, and the already-correct git rm form pass
# rule 17 — anything else falls through to the friction log
